steps.title: Use the fractional exponent rule
steps.content: A fractional exponent with numerator $1$ means a root: $$x^{1/n}=\sqrt[n]{x}.$$ Here $n=14$, so $$x^{1/14}=\sqrt[14]{x}.$$

steps.title: Check against the choices
steps.content: Choice B — $\sqrt[14]{x}$. Choice A — $\frac{1}{14}·x$ treats the exponent like multiplication by $\dfrac{1}{14}$, Choice C — $14·x$ multiplies by $14$, and Choice D — ${ \left(x\right)}^{14}$ uses the reciprocal exponent idea incorrectly by raising $x$ to the $14$th power.
